page: Amazon Web Services m8i.48xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance relies on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers exceptional throughput, achieving top-tier results in both cached and uncached memory bandwidth tests. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, drive top-tier performance in specialized tasks such as ray tracing, cryptography, and database operations. In database benchmarks, the server achieved 273.77 million operations per second in Redis SET tests.

The instance is highly optimized for large-scale enterprise workloads, high-throughput database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in CPU-based LLM inference, particularly in prompt processing for medium and large models. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy tasks. The high density of dedicated vCPUs and DDR5 memory provides qualitative cost efficiency for consolidated enterprise applications requiring massive parallel processing without the overhead of GPU accelerators.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.48xlarge is a high-capacity general-purpose server featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768 GB of DDR5 memory, delivering a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database throughput, highlighted by 273.77 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. The server's support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions accelerates cryptography, ray tracing, and CPU-based LLM inference. It is highly suited for enterprise applications, high-throughput databases, software compilation, and parallel computing workloads where high resource density and memory speed are critical.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.48xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 192 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, optimized for enterprise databases and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density x86_64 platform delivering 192 dedicated vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise databases and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Features 192 dedicated vCPUs and 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 for accelerated cryptography and AI inference
Provides 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for enterprise databases, software compilation, and web serving
